claim: Loops are autonomous agentic workflows defined by a trigger (schedule, PR open, or manual) and a verifiable goal state that the agent achieves before stopping
actionable: Design agent workflows as loops with explicit goal states rather than chained prompts, using test passing or LLM verification to determine completion
source_title: Only the best are using them...

claim: Loop engineering is expensive; costs are high today but will drop as infrastructure matures, though only elite engineers with infinite token budgets can currently experiment with this
actionable: Budget aggressively for loop engineering experiments; start with small deterministic loops (test-driven) before attempting amorphous goal loops
source_title: Only the best are using them...

claim: Loops differ from automations: automations execute scripts, while loops include decision logic to verify if the goal is reached and adapt accordingly
actionable: When building autonomous systems, implement loop semantics with explicit goal verification rather than linear automation scripts
source_title: Only the best are using them...

claim: In Claude Code, use /loop command with recurring intervals (e.g., 'loop every 5 minutes') and specify the goal - the agent will continuously run until the goal is met
actionable: Use Claude Code's /loop slash command with cron-like syntax to set up autonomous goal-driven workflows
source_title: Only the best are using them...

claim: Future engineering will shift from humans prompting agents to humans designing the autonomous factories/loops that let agents self-direct toward goals
actionable: Start learning loop design and abstract goal specification now; the future of engineering will be designing autonomous systems rather than writing prompts
source_title: Only the best are using them...
